goal: Design a persistent system for organizing personal files and documents that I will stick to.
constraints: every digital filename must begin with a five-digit category code followed immediately by an underscore.
the date element in every filename is day-first (two-digit day, two-digit month, four-digit year) and it comes straight after that initial code.
exactly six labeled physical binders; once a paper is placed in one binder, it cannot be moved to another.
digital side should be searchable and straightforward to maintain.
use open formats for long-term readability and avoid proprietary lock-in.
Automation is welcome, but ensure manual checkpoints exist for spot-checks (Checkpoint A and Checkpoint B) in the pipeline.
descriptor must be appended at the end of the filename just before the version tag and extension to provide a short human-readable context.
prefer PDF/A for long-term document images and TIFF (LZW/ZIP) for archival-quality images; maintain a lossless master and a searchable PDF for day-to-day use.
Always OCR scanned text into searchable PDFs and preserve the image layer for fidelity.
OCR confidence-based triage for receipts: assign confidence scores to fields and route low-confidence items to a Pending-Approval (manual verification) queue.

Retention policy for personal documents: keep routine utility bills for 12 months; keep tax/finance documents for 3–7 years; keep proofs of address until superseded plus 1–2 years; keep mortgage/loan/property documents for the life of the loan/ownership plus 6 years; keep warranties/receipts until warranty expires plus 1 year; keep documents related to disputes or legal matters until fully resolved and the applicable statute of limitations has passed (commonly 3–6 years, conservatively 6); keep identity/permanent documents indefinitely.
decisions: Adopt a hybrid scanning workflow (sheet-fed for standard pages; flatbed for fragile/odd-sized items).
If backlog is mostly standard loose pages, prioritize sheet-fed; otherwise prioritize flatbed.

Implement versioning policy aligned with filename rules: keep drafts with incremental vNN, generate milestone files (vNN-agreed, vNN-signed) for major events, and move signed milestones to final/archive folders with a concise change-log.
Store versions in Drafts or Workspace for active edits; move final/milestone files to binder final/archive folders; maintain a per-binder Contract-History subfolder for long-term milestones.
Preserve track changes or redlines as separate files for editable history; for binary formats rely on separate milestone copies and a change-log.
Maintain a purge-log documenting removals: filename, original path, purge date, and reason.
Create a per-binder 'Contract-History' or Archive subfolder to hold milestone versions and long-term history.
Maintain a simple change-log (CSV or plaintext) per contract listing filename, date, author, and change-summary.
Introduce an auto-suggestion mechanism for vNN with a Pending-Approval checkpoint before finalizing versions.

One-line operational rule: while active, save incremental versions (v01, v02...) in Drafts; on milestone/agreement/signing, create a milestone file (vNN-agreed or vNN-signed) and move it to the final/archive folder after updating the change-log.
Adopt bank-statement ingestion through official channels (Open Banking/PSD2 APIs, OFX, or email attachments) as preferred method.
open: Assess backlog composition: determine proportion of standard loose pages vs photos/fragile items.
Define and implement OCR confidence thresholds and a Pending-Approval queue for receipts backlog.
Define and implement a 3-2-1 backup workflow with offline encrypted drive as offsite and cloud as offsite copy.
Select and configure client-side encryption tools (VeraCrypt, LUKS, GPG/OpenPGP).
Set up and maintain SHA-256 checksums and a verification process.
Schedule annual restore tests for backups.
Establish an audit/log process for backup events.
Define and implement versioning policy (vNN, milestone versions) and require Pending-Approval for finalization.
Create and enforce a purge-log for removals.
Establish per-binder 'Contract-History' or Archive subfolder to store milestones and history.
Store a per-contract change-log (CSV or plaintext) with filename, date, author, change-summary.
Assess practicality of maintaining a simple spreadsheet inventory for important documents.
Create a printable two-page packing/checklist for scanning and secure storage.
Assess secure ingestion options for bank statements (Open Banking/PSD2 API, OFX, or email attachments) and implement in the Ingest pipeline.

Define and implement backup verification cadence: automated integrity checks weekly (or monthly), small restore spot-checks monthly or quarterly, and full restore tests annually; trigger after major changes to backup processes.

Assess handwriting recognition (HTR) options for indexing old notebooks: cloud-based handwriting OCR, Transkribus, local open-source tools (kraken, Calamari, PyLaia), and note-app ink recognition; define evaluation criteria and pilot plan.
